title: Support adding to an existing nft table or otherwise integrate with default-DROP firewalls
body:
Currently the nftables backend is unusable in proper firewalling setups that have a policy that drops all packets that are not explicitly allowed.

The reason is that podman uses its own table, and in netfilter an accept rule only terminates processing of the current table, so the drop policy rule in the firewall table would still trigger and drop all podman packets.

The iptables backend doesn't have this problem because it adds rules to the existing iptables chain, so that the ACCEPT action skips the DROP policy.

Since the iptables backend is being dropped the nftables one needs to be fixed to allow specifying an existing table (or perhaps just insert in the same tables iptables-nft uses), or providing some other way to integrate into an existing default-DROP firewall setup as additional ALLOW rules.

comments.body:
podman does not manage the firewall rules, they are created by netavark

There is already a issue over there discussing certain possibilities https://github.com/containers/netavark/issues/977
